description: Trump 2nd term began Jan 2025. Year 2 is aggressive policy implementation: Bessent at Treasury, Lutnick at Commerce. Explicitly pursuing debt monetization through controlled crises (Iran conflict → defense spending → deficit → Fed forced to accommodate). This is the most interventionist economic team since Nixon.
implication: Year 2 typically: aggressive policy changes, market volatility from uncertainty, but government backstop for markets (can't let markets crash during implementation). Pro-business but inflationary. Tax cuts + spending = deficit. Dollar weakening is POLICY, not accident.

phases.name: year1_honeymoon
phases.description: New administration agenda-setting. Executive orders. Market optimism on deregulation.
phases.market_tendency: Bullish equities, cautious bonds. Policy uncertainty creates vol.

phases.name: year2_policy_implementation
phases.duration: Jan-Dec of second year (WE ARE HERE - March 2026)
phases.description: Major policy execution. Midterm positioning. Often the most controversial period. Markets digest real policy impact vs promises.
phases.market_tendency: Higher volatility. Sector rotation. Policy beneficiaries outperform (energy, defense in current regime).

phases.name: year3_pre_election_pump
phases.description: Historically the strongest market year. Administration pumps economy for re-election. Fiscal stimulus. Easy money rhetoric.
phases.market_tendency: Strongly bullish. S&P avg +16% in year 3. The government WANTS markets higher.

phases.name: year4_election_year
phases.description: Uncertainty until election. Policy volatility. Risk premiums rise into election.
phases.market_tendency: Volatile but ultimately positive. Resolution rally post-election regardless of winner.

trump2_specifics.bessent_doctrine: Treasury Secretary Bessent's explicit goal: use geopolitical crisis to justify deficit spending → monetize debt through inflation → reduce real debt burden. Iran is the vehicle.
trump2_specifics.lutnick_commerce: Commerce Secretary Lutnick: tariffs + trade war = negotiating leverage. But also inflationary.
trump2_specifics.fed_constraint: Powell's Fed is trapped. Can't raise rates (would blow up $35T+ debt service). Can't cut (inflation still above target). Fiscal policy dominates monetary policy = fiscal dominance.
trump2_specifics.druckenmiller_connection: Druckenmiller's network IS this power structure. His framework: ride the tiger of fiscal dominance, don't fight the government. Size up when government policy is your tailwind.

key_dates_2026.impact: Defines next phase of oil thesis
If Dems win House, policy gridlock = less fiscal expansion. If GOP holds, fiscal dominance continues.
